3-Month Active Return: percent change in stock price from month t-3 to t
60-Month Active Return with 1-Month Lag: percent change in stock price from month t-61 to t-1
6-Month Active Return with 1-Month Lag: percent change in stock price from month t-7 to t-1
9-Month Active Return with 1-Month Lag: percent change in stock price from month t-10 to t-1
60-Month Alpha: intercept from regression of stock's monthly return versus S&P 500 monthly return for the past 60 months, representing risk-adjusted outperformance
Average Payable Period: average of trailing 12-month accounts payable times 365 divided by trailing 12-month cost of goods sold, indicating how long a company takes to pay suppliers
Asset Quality Index: year-over-year change of 1 minus the ratio of current assets plus PP&E to total assets, reflecting asset quality improvement or deterioration
Operating assets minus operating liabilities for the quarter, divided by lagged total assets, a balance sheet efficiency measure
Asset Composition: total current assets from the most recent quarter divided by total assets from the same quarter, indicating the proportion of current assets
Assets Turnover Ratio: trailing 12-month sales divided by most recent quarterly assets, measuring efficiency of asset usage in generating sales
Basic Defensive Interval: number of days a company can cover daily operating expenses solely from its cash and receivables
60-Month Beta: measure of stock's sensitivity to market movements, calculated as a weighted average (0.67 × 60-month beta + 0.33 × beta) using covariance of stock and S&P 500 monthly returns
Product of Beta and Sigma: product of 60-month adjusted beta and 60-month standard deviation of monthly returns, a risk metric
Book Leverage: most recent quarterly total assets divided by book equity, a leverage ratio
Capital Acquisition Ratio: trailing 12-month operating cash flow less cash dividends, divided by total expenditures in the same period, a management quality/profitability measure
Capital Expenditure-to-Total Assets: trailing 12-month capital expenditures divided by most recent quarter's total assets, a capital investment measure
TTM Capital Expenditures-to-Sales: trailing 12-month capital expenditures divided by trailing 12-month sales, indicating capital intensity relative to sales
Rate at which a company burns cash; computed as negative (operating cash flow plus investing cash flow) divided by cash and short-term investments
Cash conversion cycle in days: inventory days plus receivables days minus payables days
Cash and equivalents per share divided by trading price (cash-to-price ratio)
Cash and equivalents divided by current liabilities (cash ratio)
Cash-to-sales ratio: trailing 12-month average cash and equivalents divided by trailing 12-month sales
Total cash and equivalents divided by enterprise value
Current cash flow debt coverage: (cash flow from operations minus dividends) divided by current interest-bearing debt
Trailing 12-month investing cash flow divided by average total assets over the last four quarters
Total liabilities divided by trailing 12-month operating cash flow (cash flow leverage)
Trailing 12-month cash flow divided by average invested capital (equity, long-term debt, minority interest, and preferred stock)
Geometric growth rate of trailing 12-month sales per share over the last 12 quarters (3-year compound annual growth)
Three-year change in assets-adjusted trailing 12-month operating cash flow: current minus 12 quarters ago, scaled by average total assets
Three-year change in price-adjusted trailing 12-month cash flow per share: current minus 12 quarters ago, divided by price
Three-year change in assets-adjusted trailing 12-month EPS (before extra items): current minus 12 quarters ago, scaled by average assets per share
Three-year change in price-adjusted trailing 12-month EPS: current minus 12 quarters ago, divided by month-end trading price
Three-year change in assets-adjusted trailing 12-month free cash flow: current minus 12 quarters ago, divided by average total assets
Three-year change in price-adjusted trailing 12-month free cash flow per share: current minus 12 quarters ago, divided by month-end trading price
Three-year change in assets-adjusted trailing 12-month operating cash flow: current minus 12 quarters ago, divided by average total assets
Three-year change in price-adjusted trailing 12-month operating cash flow per share: current minus 12 quarters ago divided by trading price
Six-month nominal change in 18-month alpha; alpha is the intercept from regressing monthly returns on the S&P 500 over the past 18 months
Percent change in mean consensus long-term growth forecast compared to six months ago
Six-month nominal change in 12-month alpha; alpha is the regression intercept of monthly returns versus the S&P 500
Six-month nominal change in 36-month alpha; alpha is the regression intercept of monthly returns versus the S&P 500 over 36 months
One-year change in accounts receivable as a percentage of trailing 12-month sales; current minus four quarters ago
One-year change in assets-adjusted trailing 12-month cash flow; current minus four quarters ago scaled by average total assets
One-year change in price-adjusted trailing 12-month cash flow per share; current minus four quarters ago divided by trading price
One-year change in assets-adjusted trailing 12-month EPS (before extra items); current minus four quarters ago deflated by average assets per share
One-year change in price-adjusted trailing 12-month EPS; current minus four quarters ago divided by month-end trading price
One-year change in assets-adjusted trailing 12-month free cash flow; current minus four quarters ago divided by average total assets
One-year change in price-adjusted trailing 12-month free cash flow per share; current minus four quarters ago divided by trading price
Change in inventory as a percentage of sales over one year; calculated as latest reported inventory/sales minus same ratio from four quarters ago
Year-over-year change in net operating assets to total assets; net operating assets = short-term debt + long-term debt + preferred equity + common equity - cash
Change in net profit margin over one year; calculated as latest quarterly net profit margin minus net profit margin from four quarters ago

Change in trailing twelve-month operating cash flow, asset-adjusted, over one year; calculated as the latest TTM operating cash flow minus TTM operating cash flow from four quarters ago, divided by average total assets
Change in trailing twelve-month operating cash flow per share over one year, divided by trading price

Change in operating liability leverage over one year; operating liability leverage = 1 - (short-term debt + long-term debt + common equity + preferred equity - cash) / (total assets - cash); difference between current quarter and four quarters ago
Change in operating profit margin over one year; most recent quarterly operating profit margin minus operating profit margin from four quarters ago
Percent change in accounts receivable to current assets ratio over one year; most recent quarterly ratio minus ratio from same quarter one year ago
Difference between yearly change in quarterly SG&A expenses and yearly change in quarterly sales
Percent change in shares outstanding over one year; current vs. one year ago
Change in average trading volume over four years; difference in the most recent 6-month moving average of monthly turnover compared to the 12-month moving average four years ago
Absolute difference between yearly percent change in trailing twelve-month cost of goods sold and yearly percent change in inventory level
Sixty-month trading volume trend; slope of regression line of last 60 months' monthly trading volume against time
Stock's current book-to-price ratio minus industry average, deflated by industry standard deviation
Stock’s TTM cash flow-to-price ratio minus industry average, deflated by industry standard deviation
Stock’s TTM dividend yield minus industry average, deflated by industry standard deviation
Stock’s TTM EBITDA-to-price ratio minus industry average, deflated by industry standard deviation
Trailing twelve-month EPS-to-price ratio minus industry average, deflated by industry standard deviation
Stock’s TTM free cash flow-to-price ratio minus industry average, deflated by industry standard deviation
Stock’s TTM operating cash flow-to-price ratio minus industry average, deflated by industry standard deviation
Stock’s TTM operating cash flow-to-total assets ratio minus industry average, deflated by industry standard deviation
Stock’s TTM sales-to-price ratio minus industry average, deflated by industry standard deviation
Current assets divided by current liabilities from most recent quarter; a measure of liquidity
Security identifier, typically CUSIP or SEDOL code used to uniquely identify a stock or financial instrument
Coefficient of variation of prior 90 days closing prices; last 90 days' price standard deviation divided by mean
Ratio of 20-day price volatility to 20-day volume volatility; coefficient of variation of closing prices divided by coefficient of variation of daily trading volume for last 20 days
Long-term debt divided by book equity from most recent quarter
Sum of long-term debt and short-term interest-bearing debt divided by trailing twelve-month cash flow
Sum of trailing 12-month pretax income and interest expense divided by trailing 12-month pretax income
Trailing 12-month cash dividends divided by trailing 12-month cash flow
Dividend Coverage Ratio: Company's trailing twelve-month profit from operations divided by its annual dividend payments
TTM Dividend Yield: Trailing twelve-month dividends per share divided by month-end trading price
Change in TTM Depreciation vs. CapEx: Absolute difference between yearly percent change in depreciation expense and that of capital expenditure over trailing twelve months
TTM EBITDA-to-Enterprise Value: Trailing twelve-month earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization divided by enterprise value (EV)
TTM EBITDA-to-Price: Trailing twelve-month earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization divided by month-end trading price
EPS from Change in Effective Tax Rate: Trailing twelve-month pre-tax income per share multiplied by the change in effective tax rate over the last four quarters
Equity Turnover Ratio: Trailing twelve-month sales divided by average trailing twelve-month reported book equity
FY1 EPS Estimates Dispersion: Standard deviation of analyst earnings estimates for fiscal year one divided by mean consensus forecast
FY2 EPS Forecast Dispersion: Standard deviation of analyst earnings estimates for fiscal year two divided by mean consensus forecast
Reciprocal of Dividend Yield-adjusted PEG: Reciprocal of forward twelve-month price/earnings ratio divided by sum of forecasted long-term growth rate and indicated dividend yield
Edwards-Bell-Ohlson Value-to-Price: Firm's intrinsic valuation from EBO model divided by month-end trading price, comparing generated earnings to cost of capital
Inverse PEG Ratio: Next one year average forecasted earnings multiplied by long-term growth rate and scaled by stock price
Street Revision Magnitude: Three-month change in analysts' median forecast for fiscal year one scaled by current mean estimate
Forward 12-M EPS-to-Enterprise Value: Consensus forward twelve-month earnings per share forecast divided by sum of market value of equity per share and book value of long-term debt per share
Forward Free Cash Flow-to-Price: Next four quarters' mean consensus earnings estimate plus trailing twelve-month depreciation minus capital expenditures, divided by trading price
Product of TTM FCF Yield and Forward ROE: Trailing twelve-month free cash flow per share times next four quarters' consensus earnings forecast divided by book value per share, scaled by trading price
Forward Return on Equity: Analysts' earnings estimate for next four quarters divided by most recent reported common equity per share
Street Revision Confidence: Sum of three-month change in highest and lowest fiscal year one earnings estimates, scaled by month-end trading price
Number of Analyst Coverage: Count of analysts making earnings estimates for fiscal year one
Net Number of Revisions for Fiscal Year One: Weighted average of analyst increases minus decreases in FY1 forecasts, divided by total analyst forecasts for the month
Five-Year Relative Leading 12-M Earnings Yield: Stock's current next four quarters' consensus earnings-to-price ratio minus five-year average, scaled by five-year standard deviation
Three-Month Revision in FY1 EPS Forecasts: Change in current mean consensus forecast for fiscal year one minus three months ago, deflated by trading price
Three-Month Revision in FY2 EPS Forecasts: Change in current mean consensus earnings estimate for fiscal year two minus three months ago, deflated by trading price
Six-Month Average Revision for FY1 EPS: Average of prior six months' monthly changes in consensus analyst forecasts for fiscal year one, scaled by previous month-end price
Std Dev of FY1 EPS Estimates-to-Price: Standard deviation of analyst forecasts for fiscal year one scaled by closing price
Std Dev of FY2 EPS Estimates-to-Price: Standard deviation of analyst forecasts for fiscal year two scaled by closing price
Forecasted EPS growth for 2 years ahead: most recent consensus earnings estimate for year 2 minus year 1, divided by trading price
Trailing 12-month free cash flow divided by average book equity over the same period
Trailing 12-month free cash flow divided by average invested capital (sum of common equity, long-term debt, minority interest, preferred stock) over the same period
Trailing 12-month free cash flow divided by trailing 12-month sales
Percent change in stock price from 12 months ago to 2 months ago (momentum)
Trailing 12-month sales divided by average total fixed assets for the same period
(Current assets minus cash/equivalents) divided by (current liabilities minus short-term debt), using most recent quarter
Long-term debt divided by (total assets minus current liabilities)
Month-end price divided by highest monthly closing price in the past 12 months
Stock's lagged quarterly ROE minus industry average ROE, deflated by industry ROE standard deviation
Stock's asset-adjusted annual doubtful receivables minus industry average, deflated by industry standard deviation
Stock’s 4-week return minus industry average 4-week return, deflated by industry standard deviation
Stock’s 5-day return minus industry average 5-day return, deflated by industry standard deviation
Quarterly operating income before depreciation divided by quarterly long-term debt interest expenses
Current inventory level divided by total assets from most recent quarter
Trailing 12-month cost of goods sold divided by average inventory in the same period
Trailing 12-month EPS before extraordinary items multiplied by change in yearly sales per share growth rate, divided by trading price
Slope of regression between monthly stock trading turnover ratio and monthly stock price returns
Consensus long-term growth forecast, typically expected annual operating earnings increase over next business cycle
Monthly average of daily absolute return to daily dollar trading volume ratio (illiquidity measure)
Current market capitalization divided by number of analysts providing Fiscal Year 1 EPS estimates
Total market value plus latest reported book debt divided by market value
Book value of net assets per share minus intangibles and liabilities, divided by closing price
Absolute difference between yearly percent change in operating cash flow per share and yearly percent change in diluted EPS before extraordinary items, TTM
Quarterly cash & equivalents minus long- and short-term debt, divided by current market value
Net debt (long- plus short-term debt minus cash & equivalents) divided by the sum of net debt, preferred stock, and common stock (quarterly)
Quarterly reported net fixed assets divided by long-term debt
Net income after taxes for trailing 12 months divided by number of employees at last fiscal year end
Natural logarithm of most recent quarterly reported total assets
Natural logarithm of the cubic of stock’s total market value
Natural logarithm of the cubic of a stock's unadjusted closing price
Natural logarithm of the cubic of a company's trailing 12-month sales
Natural logarithm of the ratio of trailing 1-year average monthly volume to trailing 1-year average monthly market value of shares outstanding
Net current assets per share minus total liabilities per share, divided by price
Trailing 12-month sales divided by 4-quarter average net operating assets (net operating assets = short-term debt + long-term debt + preferred equity + common equity - cash)
Trailing 12-month NOPAT margin: (Net Income + Interest Expense × (1-Effective Tax Rate)) divided by Net Sales
Most recently reported quarterly net income after tax divided by quarterly sales
Trailing 12-month net cash flow from operations divided by average total assets
Trailing 12-month cash flow from operations divided by trailing 12-month sales
Most recently reported quarterly cash flow from operations divided by current liabilities
Trailing 12-month operating cash flow divided by average invested capital (invested capital = common equity + long-term debt + minority interest + preferred stock) for the period
1 minus the ratio of (short-term debt + long-term debt + common equity + preferred equity - cash) to (total assets - cash) for the current quarter
Difference between yearly percent change in operating income and the yearly percent change in long-term debt
Percent change in trailing 12-month operating income (from previous quarter) divided by percent change in trailing 12-month sales (from previous quarter)
Most recently reported quarterly operating income divided by quarterly sales
Moving average of last 50 days stock prices divided by moving average of last 200 days stock prices
Month-end trading price divided by most recent quarterly reported total assets per share
Current closing price divided by lowest daily low price in last 260 trading days
Percent change in most recent trailing 12-month cash flow per share compared to 12 quarters ago
Percent change in most recent trailing 12-month earnings per share compared to 12 quarters ago
Percent change in most recent trailing 12-month free cash flow per share compared to 12 quarters ago
Percent change in most recent trailing 12-month operating cash flow per share compared to 12 quarters ago
Percent change in most recent asset turnover ratio compared to 4 quarters ago; asset turnover ratio = trailing 12-month sales / average total assets
Percent change in most recent trailing 12-month cash flow per share compared to 4 quarters ago
Percent change in most recent trailing 12-month earnings per share before extra items compared to 4 quarters ago
Percent change in most recent trailing 12-month free cash flow per share compared to 4 quarters ago
Percent change in most recent trailing 12-month operating cash flow per share compared to 4 quarters ago
Growth in most recent reported quarterly total assets per share compared to four quarters ago
Growth in most recent reported quarterly sales per share compared to 4 quarters ago
Most recently reported quarterly current liabilities per share divided by closing price
Risk-adjusted PEG Ratio: Stock price divided by forward 12-month consensus earnings, scaled by average long-term growth estimate, and multiplied by 36-month beta
Pretax Return on Net Operating Assets: Trailing 12-month operating income after depreciation divided by average net operating assets
15/36 Week Stock Price Ratio: Moving average of last 15 weeks' prices divided by moving average of last 36 weeks' prices
30-75 Week Stock Price Ratio: Moving average of last 30 weeks' prices divided by moving average of last 75 weeks' prices
Quick Ratio: (Current assets minus inventories) divided by total current liabilities from the most recent quarter
Rational Decay Alpha: Historical 12-month market (S&P 500) adjusted excess return using proprietary decay function (Y-intercept from OLS regression)
Street Rating Revision: 6-month average change in analyst recommendations
R&D Intensity: Average trailing 12-month R&D expenses divided by total sales in the same period
Reinvestment Rate: (TTM earnings per share before extra items minus TTM dividends per share) divided by average book equity per share
5-year Relative Book-to-Market: Current book-to-market ratio minus 60-month average BTM, scaled by 60-month standard deviation
5-year Relative TTM Cash Flow-to-Price: Current TTM cash flow-to-price ratio minus 60-month average, scaled by 60-month standard deviation
5-year Relative TTM Dividend Yield: Current dividend yield minus 60-month average, scaled by 60-month standard deviation
5-year Relative TTM EBITDA-to-Price: Current TTM EBITDA per share-to-price minus 60-month average, scaled by 60-month standard deviation
5-year Relative TTM Earnings-to-Price: Current TTM EPS before extra items-to-price ratio minus 60-month average, scaled by 60-month standard deviation
5-year Relative TTM Free Cash Flow-to-Price: Current TTM free cash flow-to-price minus 60-month average, scaled by 60-month standard deviation
5-year Relative TTM Operating Cash Flow-to-Price: Current TTM operating cash flow-to-price minus 60-month average, scaled by 60-month standard deviation
5-year Relative TTM Sales-to-Price: Current TTM sales-to-price ratio minus 60-month average, scaled by 60-month standard deviation
Industry-adjusted 12-month Relative Price Strength: Stock's 12-month price strength minus industry average, deflated by industry standard deviation
Retained Earnings-to-Total Assets: Cumulative retained earnings from most recent quarter divided by total assets in same quarter
Regression Error of 60-Month CAPM: Standard error of regression slope for stock's last 60-month returns against S&P 500 returns
Revenue per Employee: Trailing 12-month total sales divided by number of employees at last reported fiscal year end
Return on Assets: TTM income before extra items divided by average total assets in same period
Return on Equity: TTM income before extra items divided by average common equity in same period
Return on Invested Capital: TTM net income plus interest expenses divided by average invested capital (common equity, long-term debt, minority interest, preferred stock)
26-Week Relative Price Strength: Most recent weekly closing price divided by weekly closing price 26 weeks ago
Second Preceding 6-month Return: Percent price change from month t-12 to t-7
39-Week Return with 4-week Lag: Price change from week t-43 to week t-4
Change in TTM Sales vs EPS: Absolute value of difference between yearly percent change in TTM sales per share and TTM diluted EPS percent change
5-year Sales Growth: Difference between TTM sales per share and those 20 quarters ago, deflated by average prior 20 quarters' sales per share
Change in Quarterly Sales vs Gross Margin: Difference between yearly change in most recent reported quarterly sales and yearly change in quarterly gross profit margin
Difference between the yearly percent change in trailing 12-month sales and the yearly percent change in accounts receivable
Trailing 12-month selling, general and administrative expenses divided by trailing 12-month sales
Standard deviation of a stock’s monthly returns over the prior 60 months
Skewness of the distribution of a stock's daily excess returns (relative to S&P 500) over the last 90 days, using 3*(Mean-Median)/Standard Deviation
Skewness of the distribution of a stock's daily price returns over the last 90 days, measuring lack of symmetry, calculated as 3*(Mean-Median)/Standard Deviation
4-week lagged slope coefficient from least squares regression of last 52 weeks’ weekly closing price against weekly dates
4-week lagged slope coefficient from least squares regression of last 66 weeks’ weekly closing price against weekly dates
Consensus recommendation for a company (“Street Consensus Rating”)
Difference between actual earnings per share and consensus forecasts prior to announcement, scaled by quarter-end price
Maximum growth rate a firm can sustain without increasing financial leverage
Average working capital over trailing 12 months divided by total sales in that period; working capital = inventory + accounts receivable - accounts payable
Market value of equity plus debt, divided by assets; (E+D)/A, with debt measured at book value
(Cash Flow from Operations + Interest Paid + Tax Paid) divided by (Interest + Principal Paid)
Percent change in company’s trailing 12-month total sales versus total sales one year ago
(CORREL*(260-2)^.5)/(1-CORREL^2)^.5, where CORREL is the correlation coefficient between log daily stock price and dates over the last 260 days (1-year price momentum indicator)
Difference between trailing 12-month net income and trailing 12-month operating cash flow, scaled by beginning total assets (accounting accruals)
Trailing 12-month capital expenditures per share divided by trading price
Trailing 12-month cash flow per share (net income plus depreciation) scaled by trading price
An indicator that standardizes and compares relative share price between time periods and among companies.
The company's performance in trailing 1-year before taking into account of non-recurring gain or loss.stock divided by its month-end trading price.
Trailing 12-month free cash flow for a stock divided by its enterprise value (EV = equity market value + long/short-term debt + preferred stock + minority interest - cash)
Trailing 12-month free cash flow per share divided by month-end price; FCF = operating cash flow minus capital expenditures and cash dividends
Sum of trailing 12-month growth flow value per share (R&D expenses plus reported net income) divided by month-end trading price
Trailing 12-month operating cash flow per share divided by trading price
Most recent trailing 12-month operating and non-operating income per share before income taxes and minority interest for a stock, deflated by trading price
Trailing 12-month sales for a stock divided by most recent enterprise value
Most recently reported trailing 12-month sales per share divided by month-end trading price
Difference between current accounts payable and expected accounts payable (prior year closing balance grown by trailing 12-month cost of goods sold), scaled by total assets
Difference between current accounts receivable and expected accounts receivable (prior year closing balance grown by trailing 12-month sales), scaled by total assets

Unexpected Change in Depreciation: the difference between trailing 12-month depreciation expense and the expected level (derived from prior-year closing fixed asset balance and fixed asset growth), scaled by total assets
Unexpected Change in Inventory: the difference between a company’s actual inventory level and its expected inventory level, derived from forecasts or historical trends
24-Month Value at Risk: the minimum monthly price return over the last 20 months, representing the extreme negative return in that period

24-month residual return variance: the variance of monthly residual returns over the last 24 months, where residual return equals the stock’s monthly return minus 60-month Beta times the S&P 500 monthly return
Visibility Ratio: most recent daily trading volume divided by the average daily trading volume over the previous 50 trading days
6-month moving average of the monthly trading turnover ratio, representing average trading activity over the last 6 months
Trading Turnover Ratio: monthly total trading volume divided by the most recently reported total outstanding shares

Working Capital Accruals: accrual component of earnings based on changes such as increases in accounts receivable and inventory and decreases in accounts payable, accrued liabilities, and income taxes, adjusted for asset and liability changes
Working Capital-to-Total Assets: average working capital over the trailing 12 months divided by average total assets over the same period
Working Capital to Inventory: most recent quarterly working capital divided by inventory, indicating liquidity coverage of stock levels

Year-over-year change in accruals-to-assets: the difference between scaled accruals (income before extraordinary items minus operating cash flow, divided by average assets) this period and the same quarter a year ago
Year-over-year change in current ratio: the change in current assets divided by current liabilities from the most recent quarter versus the same quarter a year ago
Year-over-year change in leverage: the change in total liabilities as a percentage of total assets from the most recent quarter versus the same quarter a year ago
Year-over-year change in gross profit margin: the change in (sales minus cost of goods sold) divided by sales from the most recent quarter versus the same quarter a year ago
Year-over-year change in return on assets: the change in trailing 12-month income before extraordinary items as a percentage of total assets versus the same quarter a year ago
Company sales exposure or proportion of total sales attributable to Asia-Pacific region
Cash Earnings Return On Equity, a measure of profitability calculated as cash earnings divided by equity
Sensitivity or beta/exposure of the company's returns or fundamentals to changes in Credit Risk Premium (spread between BAA and AAA bonds)
Debt to Assets ratio, measures leverage as total debt divided by total assets
Company sales exposure or proportion of total sales attributable to Europe, Middle East, and Africa (EMEA) region
Sensitivity or beta/exposure of the company's returns or fundamentals to changes in Housing Starts
Sensitivity or beta/exposure of the company's returns or fundamentals to changes in the inflation rate
Sensitivity or beta/exposure of the company's returns or fundamentals to changes in industrial production
Company sales exposure or proportion of total sales attributable to Latin America region
Company sales exposure or proportion of total sales attributable to North America region
Sensitivity or beta/exposure of the company's returns or fundamentals to changes in oil prices
Sensitivity or beta/exposure of the company's returns or fundamentals to changes in US Dollar value
Sensitivity or beta/exposure of the company's returns or fundamentals to changes in overall market volatility as measured by the VIX index
Sensitivity or beta/exposure of the company's returns or fundamentals to changes in the yield curve slope
Trend indicator for earnings per share (direction and persistence of recent EPS changes)
Return on equity computed using free cash flow (Free Cash Flow / Shareholders' Equity)
Measure of linkage between operating margin and sales (strength of margins relative to revenue growth)
Absolute change in trailing twelve-month operating cash flow over three years, scaled by average total assets
Change in trailing 12-month EPS (before extra items) from 12 quarters ago, divided by average total assets per share
Absolute change in trailing twelve-month free cash flow over three years, scaled by average total assets
Change in trailing 12-month free cash flow per share over 3 years, divided by month-end trading price
Change in accounts receivable to sales ratio over the past year (quarterly)
Change in trailing 12-month cash flow per share over the past year divided by trading price
Change in trailing twelve-month earnings per share (before extra items) from 4 quarters ago, scaled by average total assets per share
Change in trailing 12-month free cash flow over the past year divided by average total assets
Change in trailing 12-month free cash flow per share over the past year divided by trading price
Change in trailing twelve-month operating cash flow from 4 quarters ago, divided by average total assets
Change in trailing twelve-month operating cash flow per share from 4 quarters ago, divided by the trading price
Coefficient of variation (stability or volatility) of trailing twelve-month cash flow over the past 3 years
Coefficient of variation (a measure of stability or volatility) of trailing twelve-month earnings per share over a 3-year period
Coefficient of variation (stability or volatility) of trailing twelve-month free cash flow over a 3-year period
Coefficient of variation (stability or volatility) of trailing twelve-month operating cash flow over a 3-year period
Coefficient of variation (stability or volatility) of trailing twelve-month sales over a 3-year period
Coefficient of variation (standard deviation divided by mean) of operating income per share during last 12 quarters
TTM free cash flow divided by average book equity in the same 12-month period
Percent change in trailing twelve-month free cash flow per share over a 3-year period
Percent change in trailing twelve-month operating cash flow per share over a 3-year (12-quarter) period
Percent change in asset turnover ratio over the past year (TTM sales divided by average total assets compared to 4 quarters ago)
Percent change in trailing 12-month cash flow per share compared to 4 quarters ago
Percent change in trailing 12-month operating cash flow per share compared to 4 quarters ago
R-squared of the regression trend line for trailing twelve-month cash flow per share over the prior 12 quarters (3 years)
R-squared of the 3-year trend line for TTM EPS (regression fit quality)
R-squared of the 3-year trend line for TTM free cash flow (regression fit quality)
R-squared of the 3-year trend line for TTM operating cash flow (regression fit quality)
R-squared of the regression trend line for trailing twelve-month sales per share over the prior 12 quarters (3 years)
Standard deviation of year-over-year quarterly sales growth rate during last 12 quarters
Mean long-term EPS growth rate minus 5-year EPS growth, divided by the stability (variation) of 5-year EPS growth
Slope of the 3-year trend line for quarterly cash flow (using 4-quarter data)
Slope of trend line for TTM EPS over the past 3 years (using 4-quarter data)

Slope of the 3-year trend line for trailing twelve-month operating cash flow per share, indicating long-term growth rate
Slope of the 3-year trend line for trailing twelve-month sales per share, indicating long-term growth rate

Percent change in trailing 12-month total sales compared to one year ago
Industry Relative Book-to-Market: Stock's current book-to-price ratio minus industry average, scaled by industry standard deviation
Industry Relative TTM Cash Flow-to-Price: Stock's trailing 12-month cash flow-to-price ratio minus industry average, scaled by industry standard deviation
Industry Relative TTM Core Earnings-to-Price: Stock's trailing 12-month core earnings-to-price ratio minus industry average, scaled by industry standard deviation
Industry Relative TTM Dividend Yield: Stock's trailing 12-month dividend yield minus industry average, scaled by industry standard deviation
Industry Relative TTM EBITDA-to-Price: Stock's trailing 12-month EBITDA-to-price ratio minus industry average, scaled by industry standard deviation

Industry Relative TTM Sales-to-Price: Stock's trailing 12-month sales-to-price ratio minus industry average, scaled by industry standard deviation
Asset composition; current assets from most recent quarter divided by total assets from same quarter
Implied volatility of nearest at-the-money put options for the company
Number of days company can pay its daily operating expenses using only cash and receivables, i.e. basic defensive interval

Cash flow leverage; total liabilities divided by trailing 12-month operating cash flow
